items.title: Deputy Minister Predicts 50% Doctor No-Shows In Sabah
items.summary: Deputy Health Minister Hanifah says she expects half of medical officers offered permanent positions in Sabah later this year to reject their offers. Her forecast was based on the June intake, where only 20 of 39 doctors posted to Sabah reported for duty. The post Deputy Minister Predicts 50% Doctor No-Shows In Sabah appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: JKNS Clarifies Doctor Numbers In HTAR’s Surgery Department
items.summary: JKNS says HTAR's surgery department has 32 MOs, not 20 as reported, but admits that doctor numbers often change, in line with placements or transfers. This caused an increase in on-call frequency. HTAR is expected to receive new permanent MOs next month. The post JKNS Clarifies Doctor Numbers In HTAR’s Surgery Department appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Doctors Oppose Pharmacists’ Bid To Join Emergency Triage
items.summary: PMPASKL opposes MPS' suggestion to integrate community pharmacies into a new emergency triage system in public hospitals, saying non-critical cases aren't necessarily simple illnesses. "A pharmacy is not a clinic and a pharmacist is not a doctor." The post Doctors Oppose Pharmacists’ Bid To Join Emergency Triage appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: The Great Medical Inflation Puzzle: Who Is Really Responsible? — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam
items.summary: Every time we choose a hospital because of its marble lobby rather than its clinical outcomes, we are voting with our feet and our wallets for a system that prioritises hospitality over health care. The post The Great Medical Inflation Puzzle: Who Is Really Responsible? — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Johor’s Cardiothoracic Service Can’t Continue Like This — Medical Officer
items.summary: Johor Bahru's cardiothoracic service is divided between HSA and HSI. MOs and specialists travel repeatedly between both hospitals that are 20km apart. Two members of our department were involved in road accidents recently. Infrastructure is another issue. The post Johor’s Cardiothoracic Service Can’t Continue Like This — Medical Officer appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Mandatory National Health Insurance ‘Not Politically Right’: Dzulkefly
items.summary: Dr Dzul says Putrajaya isn't planning to introduce mandatory national health insurance, likening it to a "double war" on top of general taxation that funds public health care. The Madani government wants to "mainstream" private health insurance instead. The post Mandatory National Health Insurance ‘Not Politically Right’: Dzulkefly appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Urea Prices Jumped 63% Amid West Asia Conflict: Mat Sabu
items.summary: Mohamad Sabu says urea prices jumped 63% from Feb to May this year due to the Persian Gulf conflict that directly affected the cost of agricultural inputs. Domestic food inflation reached 1.4% in May, but rice, chicken and egg supplies are sufficient. The post Urea Prices Jumped 63% Amid West Asia Conflict: Mat Sabu appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Health Minister Must Act On HTAR Klang Crisis — Dr Halimah Ali
items.summary: MOH should urgently conduct an independent assessment of workforce adequacy and workload within HTAR’s surgical services. If our surgeons are telling us that they have reached the limit, the responsible response is to act before patients pay the price. The post Health Minister Must Act On HTAR Klang Crisis — Dr Halimah Ali appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: DVS Flags Salmonella Infantis Among Top Five Serovars Found In Healthy Poultry
items.summary: Salmonella Infantis is now among the top five Salmonella serovars isolated from healthy poultry in Malaysia, says DVS. It also revealed that 90% of broiler chicken and 93% of layer chicken Salmonella isolates were multidrug-resistant in 2025. The post DVS Flags Salmonella Infantis Among Top Five Serovars Found In Healthy Poultry appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Behind Closed Doors In HSAJB’s Emergency Department — Houseman
items.summary: A houseman claims that HSAJB's emergency department suffers from a lack of facilities like ECG machines, vital sign monitoring, and oxygen ports, besides lack of privacy when attending to patients, unhelpful support staff, and deplorable toilet conditions. The post Behind Closed Doors In HSAJB’s Emergency Department — Houseman appeared first on CodeBlue .
